draft.introduction: An alphabetic acrostic hymn declaring God’s kingdom and compassion. “The Lord is near to all who call on him” became foundational for both Jewish and Christian prayer theology.

translation.text: I will extol you, my God and King, and bless your name forever and ever. Great is the Lord, and greatly to be praised, and his greatness is unsearchable. The Lord is gracious and merciful, slow to anger and abounding in steadfast love.
translation.original_text: ὑψώσω σε ὁ θεός μου ὁ βασιλεύς

translation.text: Your kingdom is an everlasting kingdom. The Lord is near to all who call on him, to all who call on him in truth. He fulfills the desire of those who fear him; he also hears their cry and saves them.
translation.original_text: ἡ βασιλεία σου βασιλεία πάντων τῶν αἰώνων

translator_notes.note: “The Lord is near” (ἐγγὺς κύριος) uses the same word Paul employs in Philippians 4:5. The nearness of God is not merely spatial but relational—a theme central to theosis.
